description:
You are a bioengineer preparing to heterologously express the MaSp1 (Major ampullate spidroin 1) dragline silk gene from the golden silk orb-weaver spider. Before ordering a synthetic construct, you need to document the native codon usage bias. The partial genome sequence is at ~/UGENE_Data/spidroin/masp1_sequence.gb.

Perform the following tasks using UGENE:

1. Open ~/UGENE_Data/spidroin/masp1_sequence.gb.
2. Isolate the annotated Coding Sequence (CDS) and translate it into a protein sequence.
3. Export the translated protein sequence as a FASTA file to ~/UGENE_Data/spidroin/results/masp1_protein.fasta.
4. Use UGENE's sequence analysis tools to compute the Codon Usage table specifically for the CDS.
5. Export or save the resulting codon usage table as a CSV or plain text file to ~/UGENE_Data/spidroin/results/masp1_codon_usage.csv.
6. Analyze the codon usage data and write a summary report to ~/UGENE_Data/spidroin/results/masp1_analysis_report.txt. The report MUST contain exactly these four lines (replace the bracketed placeholders with your findings):

Protein length: [X] amino acids
Most abundant amino acid: [Name]
Most frequent Glycine codon: [XYZ]
Most frequent Alanine codon: [XYZ]
